2948.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Sirin (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The first one to use analogical reasoning was Iblis. The sun and moon were never worshipped except through analogical reasoning.'
2948.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Sirin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Yang pertama menggunakan penalaran analogis adalah Iblis. Matahari dan bulan tidak pernah disembah kecuali melalui penalaran analogis.'

2949.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Hasan al-Basri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he recited this verse: 'You created me from fire, and You created him from clay' (Quran 7:12). He said: 'Iblis used analogical reasoning, and he was the first to use it.'
2949.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Hasan al-Bashri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia membaca ayat ini: 'Engkau menciptakanku dari api dan Engkau menciptakannya dari tanah' (QS. Al-A'raf: 12). Ia berkata: 'Iblis menggunakan penalaran analogis, dan ia adalah yang pertama menggunakannya.'

2950.iwh_en: It was narrated from Masruq (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I fear — or I am afraid — that if I use analogical reasoning, my foot will slip.'
2950.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Masruq (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku takut — atau aku khawatir — bahwa jika aku menggunakan penalaran analogis, kakiku akan tergelincir.'

2951.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'By Allah — if you resort to analogical reasoning, you will certainly make the lawful forbidden and make the forbidden lawful.'
2951.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Demi Allah — jika kalian menggunakan penalaran analogis, kalian pasti akan mengharamkan yang halal dan menghalalkan yang haram.'

2952.iwh_en: It was narrated from Amir al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'How much I dislike the expression "what do you think" — a man asks his companion "what do you think" — and he would not use analogical reasoning.'
2952.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Amir al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Betapa aku membenci ungkapan "apa pendapatmu" — seorang pria bertanya kepada temannya "apa pendapatmu" — dan ia tidak menggunakan penalaran analogis.'

2953.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Zibriqan (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Abu Wa'il forbade me from sitting with the people who use "what do you think" reasoning.'
2953.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Zibriqan (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Abu Wa'il melarangku duduk bersama orang-orang yang menggunakan penalaran "apa pendapatmu".'

2954.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'If these people had been in the time of the Prophet ﷺ, most of the Quran would have been revealed in the form of "They ask you" and "They ask you."'
2954.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Seandainya orang-orang ini hidup di zaman Nabi ﷺ, sebagian besar Al-Quran akan diturunkan dalam bentuk "Mereka bertanya kepadamu" dan "Mereka bertanya kepadamu."'

2955.iwh_en: It was narrated from Maymun Abu Hamzah (may Allah be pleased with him) that Ibrahim al-Nakha'i said to him: 'O Abu Hamzah, by Allah I have spoken — but had I found a way out I would not have spoken. Indeed a time in which I am a jurist among the people is indeed a bad time.'
2955.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Maimun Abu Hamzah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Ibrahim al-Nakha'i berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Abu Hamzah, demi Allah aku telah berbicara — tetapi seandainya aku menemukan jalan keluar aku tidak akan berbicara. Sesungguhnya suatu zaman di mana aku menjadi ahli fiqh di antara manusia adalah zaman yang buruk.'

2956.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mujahid (may Allah be pleased with him) that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'Beware of measuring one thing against another — meaning: in speech.'
2956.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mujahid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Waspadalah terhadap mengukur satu hal dengan hal lain — artinya: dalam perkataan.'

2957.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was present with Shurayh when a man from the Murad tribe came to him and said: O Abu Umayya, what is the blood money for fingers? He said: Ten, ten camels each. He said: Glory be to Allah — are the index finger and the little finger the same? Shurayh said: What a pity for you — the Sunnah preceded your analogy. The Sunnah is that the index finger and the little finger are each worth ten camels.'
2957.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku hadir bersama Syuraih ketika seorang pria dari suku Murad datang kepadanya dan berkata: Wahai Abu Umayya, apa diyat untuk jari-jari? Ia berkata: Sepuluh, sepuluh unta masing-masing. Ia berkata: Subhanallah — apakah jari telunjuk dan jari kelingking sama? Syuraih berkata: Kasihan kamu — Sunnah mendahului analogimu. Sunnah menetapkan bahwa jari telunjuk dan jari kelingking masing-masing bernilai sepuluh unta.'

2958.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Quran will be opened to the people until even women, children, and men will read it. Then a man will say: I have read the Quran but the people do not follow me — so let me innovate something for them that they will follow. So beware of what he innovates — for everything he innovates is an error.'
2958.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adz ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Al-Quran akan terbuka bagi manusia hingga bahkan wanita, anak-anak, dan pria akan membacanya. Kemudian seorang pria akan berkata: Aku telah membaca Al-Quran tetapi orang-orang tidak mengikutiku — maka biarkan aku memperkenalkan sesuatu yang baru untuk mereka yang akan mereka ikuti. Maka waspadalah terhadap apa yang ia perkenalkan — karena setiap yang ia perkenalkan adalah kesesatan.'

2959.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Sha'bi (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Whatever these people narrate to you from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — take it. And whatever they say based on their own opinions — throw it in the garbage.'
2959.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Sya'bi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Apapun yang orang-orang ini ceritakan kepadamu dari Rasulullah ﷺ — ambillah. Dan apapun yang mereka katakan berdasarkan pendapat mereka sendiri — buanglah ke tong sampah.'

2960.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdah ibn Abi Lubabah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'All I ask of the people of my time is that they neither ask me nor I ask them — for one of them only says: "This is my opinion and this is your opinion." I have never seen anyone who was more learned than the companions of the Prophet ﷺ, nor anyone who gave fewer opinions.'
2960.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdah ibn Abi Lubabah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Yang aku minta dari orang-orang zamanku hanyalah bahwa mereka tidak bertanya kepadaku dan aku tidak bertanya kepada mereka — karena salah seorang dari mereka hanya berkata: "Ini pendapatku dan ini pendapatmu." Aku tidak pernah melihat siapapun yang lebih berpengetahuan dari para sahabat Nabi ﷺ, dan tidak ada yang lebih sedikit memberikan pendapat pribadi.'

2961.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ drew a line for us one day, then said: This is the path of Allah — straight. Then he drew lines to the right and left of it and said: These are other paths. On each of these paths is a devil calling people to it. Then he recited: And verily, this is My straight path, so follow it, and do not follow the other paths, for they will separate you from His path. (Quran 6:153)'
2961.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ membuat garis untuk kami pada suatu hari, kemudian bersabda: Ini adalah jalan Allah — lurus. Kemudian beliau membuat garis-garis di kanan dan kirinya dan bersabda: Inilah jalan-jalan lain. Di setiap jalan-jalan ini terdapat setan yang menyeru manusia ke arahnya. Kemudian beliau membaca: Dan sungguh, inilah jalan-Ku yang lurus, maka ikutilah, dan jangan ikuti jalan-jalan yang lain, karena jalan-jalan itu akan memisahkan kalian dari jalan-Nya. (QS. Al-An'am: 153)'

2962.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mujahid (may Allah be pleased with him) regarding the verse: '...and do not follow the other paths' (Quran 6:153): he said: 'The other paths are innovations and doubtful matters.'
2962.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mujahid (radhiyallahu 'anhu) mengenai ayat: '...dan janganlah kamu mengikuti jalan-jalan yang lain' (QS. Al-An'am: 153): ia berkata: 'Jalan-jalan lain itu adalah bid'ah dan perkara-perkara yang samar.'

2963.iwh_en: It was narrated from Amr ibn Yahya's father through his grandfather (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We used to sit at the door of Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) before the Fajr prayer. When he came out, we would walk with him to the mosque. One day Abu Musa al-Ash'ari came to us and said: Has Abu Abd al-Rahman come out yet? We said: No. So he sat with us until he came out. When he came out, we all rose to greet him. Abu Musa said: O Abu Abd al-Rahman, I saw something in the mosque just now that I disapprove of — but praise be to Allah, I only saw good. He said: What was it? He said: If you live you will see it. He said: I saw people in the mosque sitting in circles, waiting for the prayer. In each circle there was a man, and in their hands were pebbles. The man would say: Say Allahu Akbar one hundred times — and they would say it one hundred times. Say La ilaha illAllah one hundred times — and they would say it. Say SubhanAllah one hundred times — and they would say it.'
2963.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ayah Amr ibn Yahya melalui kakeknya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami biasa duduk di pintu Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) sebelum shalat Subuh. Ketika beliau keluar, kami berjalan bersamanya ke masjid. Suatu hari Abu Musa al-Asy'ari datang kepada kami dan berkata: Apakah Abu Abd al-Rahman sudah keluar? Kami berkata: Belum. Maka ia duduk bersama kami sampai beliau keluar. Ketika beliau keluar, kami semua berdiri menyambutnya. Abu Musa berkata: Wahai Abu Abd al-Rahman, aku melihat sesuatu di masjid tadi yang aku tidak setujui — tetapi alhamdulillah, aku hanya melihat kebaikan. Beliau berkata: Apa itu? Ia berkata: Jika kamu masih hidup kamu akan melihatnya. Ia berkata: Aku melihat orang-orang di masjid duduk dalam lingkaran, menunggu shalat. Di setiap lingkaran ada seorang pria, dan di tangan mereka ada batu kerikil. Pria itu berkata: Ucapkan Allahu Akbar seratus kali — dan mereka mengucapkannya seratus kali. Ucapkan La ilaha illAllah seratus kali — dan mereka mengucapkannya. Ucapkan SubhanAllah seratus kali — dan mereka mengucapkannya.'

2964.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) through Abu Abd al-Rahman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Follow and do not innovate — for you have been given enough.'
2964.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui Abu Abd al-Rahman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Ikutilah dan jangan berinovasi — karena kalian telah diberi kecukupan.'

2965.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressed us in a sermon and said: 'The best of speech is the Book of Allah, and the best of guidance is the guidance of Muhammad ﷺ. The worst of affairs are newly invented matters, and every innovation is misguidance.'
2965.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Abdullah al-Anshari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah kepada kami dan bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik perkataan adalah Kitabullah, dan sebaik-baik petunjuk adalah petunjuk Muhammad ﷺ. Seburuk-buruk perkara adalah yang baru dibuat-buat, dan setiap bid'ah adalah kesesatan.'

2966.iwh_en: It was narrated from Bilad ibn Ismah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him), when the evening of Thursday came — the night of Friday — saying: 'We are in the best of times. No night comes without the next night being worse than it, until the Day of Resurrection. I heard this from your Prophet ﷺ.'
2966.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Bilad ibn Ismah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu), ketika sore hari Kamis tiba — malam Jumat — berkata: 'Kami berada di zaman terbaik. Tidak ada malam yang datang kecuali malam berikutnya lebih buruk darinya, hingga Hari Kiamat. Aku mendengar ini dari Nabi kalian ﷺ.'

2967.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Sirin (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'No man has adopted a religious innovation and then returned to the Sunnah.'
2967.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Sirin (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Tidak ada seorang pria pun yang mengambil bid'ah dalam agama kemudian kembali kepada Sunnah.'

2968.iwh_en: It was narrated from Thawban (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'What I fear most for my community is the misguiding imams. And when the sword is set down among my community, it will never be lifted from it until the Day of Resurrection. And the Hour will not be established until communities of my community join the polytheists, and until communities of my community worship idols. And there will certainly be in my community thirty great liars, each claiming to be a prophet — while I am the Seal of the Prophets; there is no prophet after me. A group from my community will always remain on the truth, manifest — those who oppose them will not harm them — until Allah's decree comes.'
2968.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Tsauban (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Yang paling aku takutkan bagi umatku adalah para imam yang menyesatkan. Dan ketika pedang diletakkan di antara umatku, ia tidak akan pernah diangkat darinya hingga Hari Kiamat. Dan Kiamat tidak akan terjadi sampai komunitas-komunitas dari umatku bergabung dengan kaum musyrik, dan sampai komunitas-komunitas dari umatku menyembah berhala. Dan sesungguhnya dalam umatku akan ada tiga puluh pembohong besar, masing-masing mengklaim sebagai nabi — sementara aku adalah Penutup para Nabi; tidak ada nabi setelahku. Sekelompok dari umatku akan selalu berada di atas kebenaran, tampak nyata — mereka yang menentang mereka tidak akan membahayakan mereka — sampai ketentuan Allah datang.'

2969.iwh_en: It was narrated from Hayyah bint Abi Hayyah (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'A man came to us at al-Zahir and said: May I sit with you while you eat? They said: Yes. He sat and ate with them. When he was finished, he said: I am the Messenger of Allah to you. They said: What is your message? He said: Three things: Do not perform dhikr after the obligatory prayers, do not supplicate with a specific supplication in the final sitting of the prayer, and never seek closeness to Allah through fasting, for it will not draw you closer to Him. So some of them went to Hudhayfah (may Allah be pleased with him) and told him. He said: These three things are the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — and the man was a liar.'
2969.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Hayyah binti Abi Hayyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Seorang pria datang kepada kami di al-Zahir dan berkata: Bolehkah aku duduk bersama kalian saat makan? Mereka berkata: Ya. Ia duduk dan makan bersama mereka. Ketika selesai, ia berkata: Aku adalah utusan Allah untuk kalian. Mereka berkata: Apa pesanmu? Ia berkata: Tiga hal: Jangan berdzikir setelah shalat wajib, jangan berdoa dengan doa tertentu pada duduk terakhir shalat, dan jangan pernah mendekatkan diri kepada Allah melalui puasa, karena puasa tidak akan mendekatkanmu kepada-Nya. Maka beberapa dari mereka pergi kepada Hudzaifah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dan memberitahunya. Ia berkata: Ketiga hal ini adalah Sunnah Rasulullah ﷺ — dan pria itu adalah pendusta.'

2970.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The thing I fear most for you are the misguiding imams.'
2970.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Hal yang paling aku khawatirkan bagi kalian adalah para imam yang menyesatkan.'

2971.iwh_en: It was narrated from Qays ibn Abi Hazim (may Allah be pleased with him) that Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him) entered upon a woman from the Ahmas tribe named Zaynab. He saw that she was not speaking. He asked: What is wrong with her — why does she not speak? He was told: She has made a vow of silence. He said: This is not permissible. Speak — for this is not lawful. Silence is part of the acts of the pre-Islamic period of ignorance. So she spoke.',
2971.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Qais ibn Abi Hazim (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Abu Bakr (radhiyallahu 'anhu) masuk menemui seorang wanita dari suku Ahmas bernama Zainab. Ia melihat bahwa ia tidak berbicara. Ia bertanya: Ada apa dengannya — mengapa ia tidak berbicara? Ia diberitahu: Ia telah bernazar untuk diam. Ia berkata: Ini tidak diperbolehkan. Berbicaralah — karena ini tidak halal. Diam adalah bagian dari perbuatan masa Jahiliyah. Maka ia pun berbicara.

2972.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aidhah (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I saw Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) giving advice to men and women and saying: Whoever is upon a middle course while following the Sunnah is better than whoever strives hard in following an innovation.'
2972.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aidzah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) memberi nasihat kepada pria dan wanita dan berkata: Siapapun yang berada di jalan tengah sambil mengikuti Sunnah lebih baik dari siapapun yang berusaha keras mengikuti bid'ah.'

2973.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ziyad ibn Hudayr (may Allah be pleased with him) that Umar (may Allah be pleased with him) said to him: 'Do you know what destroys Islam?' I said: No. He said: 'What destroys it is the slip of a scholar, the disputing of a hypocrite over the Book of Allah, and the ruling of misguiding leaders.'
2973.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ziyad ibn Hudir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata kepadanya: 'Tahukah kamu apa yang menghancurkan Islam?' Aku berkata: Tidak. Ia berkata: 'Yang menghancurkannya adalah tergelincirnya seorang ulama, perdebatan seorang munafik tentang Kitabullah, dan putusan para pemimpin yang menyesatkan.'

2974.iwh_en: It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Do not sit with people of controversy — for indeed they wade into the verses of Allah.'
2974.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Muhammad ibn Ali (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Jangan duduk bersama orang-orang yang suka berdebat — karena sesungguhnya mereka menceburkan diri ke dalam ayat-ayat Allah.'

2975.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Hasan al-Basri (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Your Sunnah — by the One besides Whom there is no deity — lies between those who go to excess and those who fall short. So be patient with it, may Allah have mercy on you — for indeed the people of the Sunnah were the fewest of people in earlier times and they will be fewer still at the end of time.'
2975.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Hasan al-Bashri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Sunnah kalian — demi Dzat yang tiada ilah selain-Nya — terletak di antara mereka yang berlebih-lebihan dan mereka yang kurang. Maka bersabarlah dengannya, semoga Allah merahmati kalian — karena sesungguhnya orang-orang Sunnah adalah yang paling sedikit di masa-masa awal dan mereka akan lebih sedikit lagi di akhir zaman.'

2976.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Moderation in following the Sunnah is better than striving hard in following an innovation.'
2976.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Bersikap sederhana dalam mengikuti Sunnah lebih baik dari bersungguh-sungguh dalam mengikuti bid'ah.'

2977.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibrahim al-Nakha'i (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I have known people such that if even one of them had not gone beyond cutting a fingernail in a certain way, I would not go beyond it either. It is sufficient disgrace for a people that their actions are departed from.'
2977.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibrahim al-Nakha'i (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku telah mengenal orang-orang sedemikian rupa sehingga jika salah satu dari mereka tidak melampaui memotong kuku dengan cara tertentu, aku pun tidak akan melampauinya. Sudah cukup memalukan bagi suatu kaum bahwa perbuatan-perbuatan mereka ditinggalkan.'

2978.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ata' (may Allah be pleased with him) regarding the verse: 'Obey Allah and obey the Messenger and those in authority among you' (Quran 4:59): He said: '"Those in authority" refers to the people of knowledge and fiqh (jurisprudence), and obedience to the Messenger means following the Book and the Sunnah.'
2978.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Atha' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) mengenai ayat: 'Taatilah Allah dan taatilah Rasul dan ulil amri di antara kamu' (QS. An-Nisa: 59): Ia berkata: '"Ulil amri" merujuk pada para ahli ilmu dan fiqh (yurisprudensi), dan ketaatan kepada Rasul berarti mengikuti Kitab dan Sunnah.'

2979.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibrahim ibn Adham (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I asked Ibn Shubrumah about something — I had a difficult question. I said: May Allah have mercy on you — look into it. He said: When the path is clear for me and I find the transmitted narration, I have no need for my personal opinion.'
2979.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibrahim ibn Adham (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku bertanya kepada Ibnu Shubrumah tentang sesuatu — aku memiliki pertanyaan yang sulit. Aku berkata: Semoga Allah merahmatimu — perhatikanlah itu. Ia berkata: Ketika jalan sudah jelas bagiku dan aku menemukan riwayat yang diriwayatkan, aku tidak memerlukan pendapat pribadinya.'

2980.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'Learn knowledge and teach it to the people. Learn the laws of inheritance and teach them to the people. Learn the Quran and teach it to the people — for I am a man who will be taken away, and knowledge will be lifted and trials will appear until two people will disagree about an obligation, and they will find no one to settle it for them.'
2980.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Mas'ud (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepadanya: 'Pelajarilah ilmu dan ajarkanlah kepada manusia. Pelajarilah hukum-hukum waris dan ajarkanlah kepada manusia. Pelajarilah Al-Quran dan ajarkanlah kepada manusia — karena aku adalah seorang pria yang akan dipanggil pergi, dan ilmu akan diangkat dan fitnah akan muncul sampai dua orang berselisih tentang suatu kewajiban, dan mereka tidak menemukan siapapun untuk menyelesaikannya bagi mereka.'

2981.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent Mu'adh ibn Jabal and others to Yemen to teach the people. He said: 'Learn knowledge before it is lifted, and its being lifted means that it departs with its people. A scholar is not questioned about the reason he learned knowledge — you must learn it. Your reward in learning it is great.'
2981.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah ibn Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengutus Mu'adz ibn Jabal dan orang-orang lainnya ke Yaman untuk mengajar manusia. Beliau bersabda: 'Pelajarilah ilmu sebelum ia diangkat, dan diangkatnya ilmu berarti ia pergi bersama para pemiliknya. Seorang ulama tidak ditanya tentang alasan ia mempelajari ilmu — kalian harus mempelajarinya. Pahala kalian dalam mempelajarinya sangat besar.'

2982.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurayrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'It was said: O Messenger of Allah, who is the most honorable of people? He said: The most pious among them. They said: We do not ask about that. He said: Then the most honorable is Joseph, the Prophet of Allah, son of the Prophet of Allah, son of the Prophet of Allah, son of the close friend of Allah. They said: We do not ask about that. He said: Are you then asking about the best lineages of the Arabs? The best of them in the era of ignorance are the best of them in Islam, if they have understanding.'
2982.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Dikatakan: Ya Rasulullah, siapakah orang paling mulia? Beliau bersabda: Yang paling bertakwa di antara mereka. Mereka berkata: Kami tidak menanyakan tentang itu. Beliau bersabda: Maka yang paling mulia adalah Yusuf, Nabi Allah, putra Nabi Allah, putra Nabi Allah, putra kekasih Allah. Mereka berkata: Kami tidak menanyakan tentang itu. Beliau bersabda: Apakah kalian kemudian bertanya tentang keturunan terbaik dari orang-orang Arab? Yang terbaik di antara mereka pada masa Jahiliyah adalah yang terbaik di antara mereka dalam Islam, jika mereka memiliki pemahaman.'

2983.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: If Allah wills good for a person, He gives him understanding in religion. And I am only a distributor — Allah is the One who gives. This community will not cease to remain upright in Allah's command, unharmed by those who oppose them, until the decree of Allah comes.'
2983.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Jika Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi seseorang, Dia memberinya pemahaman dalam agama. Dan aku hanyalah seorang yang membagi-bagikan — Allah-lah Yang memberi. Komunitas ini tidak akan berhenti tetap lurus dalam perintah Allah, tidak terganggu oleh mereka yang menentang mereka, sampai ketentuan Allah datang.'

2984.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If Allah wills good for a person, He gives him understanding in religion.'
2984.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi seseorang, Dia memberinya pemahaman dalam agama.'

2985.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'awiyah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: If Allah wills good for a person, He gives him understanding in religion.'
2985.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'awiyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: Jika Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi seseorang, Dia memberinya pemahaman dalam agama.'

2986.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jubayr ibn Mut'im (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If Allah wills good for a person, He gives him understanding in religion and inspires him with right guidance.'
2986.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jubair ibn Muth'im (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika Allah menghendaki kebaikan bagi seseorang, Dia memberinya pemahaman dalam agama dan mengilhaminya dengan petunjuk yang benar.'

2987.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jubayr ibn Mut'im (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood up in al-Khayf mosque of Mina and said: 'May Allah cause to flourish the man who hears my words, memorizes them, and conveys them. For perhaps one who conveys knowledge is more knowledgeable than the one who conveys it to. Three things will not cause the heart of a Muslim to harbor hatred: sincerity of deed for Allah's sake, giving sincere counsel to those in authority, and holding fast to the community — for their prayer encompasses those behind them.'
2987.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jubair ibn Muth'im (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri di masjid al-Khaif di Mina dan bersabda: 'Semoga Allah menjadikan bersinar orang yang mendengar perkataanku, menghafalnya, dan menyampaikannya. Karena mungkin saja orang yang menyampaikan ilmu lebih berpengetahuan daripada orang yang menyampaikannya kepadanya. Tiga hal tidak akan menyebabkan hati seorang Muslim menyimpan kebencian: ketulusan amal karena Allah, memberikan nasihat tulus kepada mereka yang berwenang, dan berpegang teguh pada jemaah — karena doa mereka mencakup orang-orang di belakang mereka.'

2988.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aban ibn Uthman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Zayd ibn Thabit (may Allah be pleased with him) came out from the presence of Marwan ibn al-Hakam at midday. I said: He would not have sent for him at this hour for nothing. I asked him and he said: He asked me about some matters regarding the Sunnah, and I informed him.'
2988.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aban ibn Utsman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Zaid ibn Tsabit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) keluar dari hadapan Marwan ibn al-Hakam pada tengah hari. Aku berkata: Ia tidak akan memanggilnya pada jam ini tanpa alasan. Aku bertanya kepadanya dan ia berkata: Ia bertanya kepadaku tentang beberapa perkara mengenai Sunnah, dan aku memberitahunya.'

2989.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu al-Darda' (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ addressed them and said: 'Indeed the scholars are the heirs of the prophets. The prophets left no gold or silver as inheritance — they left knowledge. So whoever takes it has taken a generous portion.'
2989.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu al-Darda' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah kepada mereka dan bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya para ulama adalah pewaris para nabi. Para nabi tidak meninggalkan warisan berupa emas atau perak — mereka meninggalkan ilmu. Maka siapapun yang mengambilnya telah mengambil bagian yang murah hati.'

2990.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever intentionally lies about me — let him take his seat in the Fire.'
2990.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Siapapun yang sengaja berdusta atasku — hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'

2991.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with him and his father) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'Whoever intentionally lies about me — let him take his seat in the Fire.'
2991.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Siapapun yang sengaja berdusta atasku — hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'

2992.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Zubayr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Be careful in narrating from me — except for what you are certain of. And whoever lies about me intentionally — let him take his seat in the Fire.'
2992.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Zubair (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Berhati-hatilah dalam meriwayatkan dariku — kecuali apa yang kamu yakini. Dan siapapun yang sengaja berdusta atasku — hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'

2993.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ya'la ibn Murrah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever intentionally lies about me — let him take his seat in the Fire.'
2993.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ya'la ibn Murrah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Siapapun yang sengaja berdusta atasku — hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'

2994.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Were it not that I fear making an error, I would have narrated to you things that I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ or from the Prophet ﷺ.'
2994.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Seandainya aku tidak takut melakukan kesalahan, aku pasti akan menceritakan kepada kalian hal-hal yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ atau dari Nabi ﷺ.'

2995.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ that he said: 'Whoever intentionally lies about me — let him take his seat in the Fire.'
2995.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Siapapun yang sengaja berdusta atasku — hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'

2996.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say from the pulpit: O people, beware of narrating excessively from me — for whoever says something about me must say only the truth. And whoever intentionally attributes a lie to me — let him take his seat in the Fire.'
2996.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda dari mimbar: Wahai manusia, waspadalah terhadap berlebihan dalam meriwayatkan dariku — karena siapapun yang mengatakan sesuatu tentangku harus mengatakan yang benar saja. Dan siapapun yang sengaja mengatributkan kebohongan kepadaku — hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'

2997.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever intentionally lies about me — let him take his seat in the Fire.'
2997.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Siapapun yang sengaja berdusta atasku — hendaklah ia mengambil tempatnya di neraka.'
